# Circular Motion & Trigonometry

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this chapter, you will be able to:

- Describe circular motion using angle and radius.
- Set up and solve right-triangle trigonometry problems.
- Evaluate trigonometric functions using the unit circle.
- Recognize core trigonometric identities.

---

## Circular Motion Basics

*(Seasonal daylight cycles, tidal motion, satellite orbit basics, wind turbine blade motion.)*

## Right-Triangle Trigonometry

*(SOH-CAH-TOA; setting up and solving right-triangle problems.)*

### Concept Check: Right-Triangle Trig Setup {-}

*(To be drafted.)*

## The Unit Circle

*(Standard angles; sine/cosine/tangent values.)*

## Core Trig Identities (Introduction)

*(Pythagorean identity and friends, introduced lightly.)*

### Concept Check: Unit Circle Values {-}

*(To be drafted.)*

## Practice: Modeling a Rotational/Cyclical Process

*(To be drafted.)*

## Skills Drills

### Evaluating Trig Functions at Standard Angles

*(To be drafted.)*

---

## Midterm 2 Preview {-}

This chapter concludes the material covered on Midterm 2 (Chapters 5–8). See the Midterm 2 Review chapter in the back matter for practice materials.
